clusters.headline: Which teams are in World Cup last-32 knockouts, and what is the schedule?
clusters.summary: All the qualified teams and match schedule for the round of 32 at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which begins on Sunday.

clusters.headline: Iran war day 121: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait as US strikes near Hormuz
clusters.summary: US strikes Iran for a second day near Strait of Hormuz as Bahrain sounds sirens and Kuwait activates air defences.

clusters.headline: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
clusters.summary: Kinshasa accuses its neighbour of committing various violations since the 1994 Rwanda genocide.

clusters.headline: Burkina Faso severs diplomatic ties with France
clusters.summary: The West African country's junta accused the former colonial power of undermining its national interests.

clusters.headline: Nigeria: President Tinubu Approves National Health Technology and Data Analytics Office, Appoints Obi Adigwe National Coordinator
clusters.summary: [State Department] President Bola Tinubu has approved the establishment of the National Health Technology and Data Analytics Office (NHTDAO) and appointed Dr Obi Adigwe as the pioneer National Coordinator.

clusters.headline: Africa: Blue Transformation: The future of food will be shaped by water -- Africa at the heart of FAO's strategy
Kenya: DPP Closes Case in Sh8.2bn Meth Bust As Six Iranians Await Fate in Landmark Drug Trafficking Trial
clusters.summary: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- The Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions (DPP) has closed its case against six Iranian nationals accused of trafficking more than one tonne of methamphetamine worth Sh8.2 billion, paving the way for the court to determine whether they have a case to answer.

clusters.headline: Uganda: Cross-Border Businesses Crippled As Traders Appeal for Reopening of Mpondwe-Lhubiriha Border
clusters.summary: [Nile Post] Traders operating at the Mpondwe-Lhubiriha border in Kasese District are facing mounting financial losses following the temporary closure of the border point, which was introduced several weeks ago as part of government measures to contain the spread of the Ebola outbreak.

clusters.headline: Kenya: Plea Deferred for 8 Utumishi Girls Students As Court Orders Mental Assessment in Murder Case
clusters.summary: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- The High Court in Kibera has deferred plea-taking for eight Utumishi Girls Academy students charged with the murder of sixteen schoolmates who died in a dormitory fire last month, ordering that they undergo mental and age assessments before standing trial.

clusters.headline: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
clusters.summary: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.

clusters.headline: A historian analyzes whether Ukraine has a localized advantage in modern combat power
clusters.summary: NPR's Scott Simon talks with strategic studies professor Phillips O'Brien of St Andrew's University in Scotland about whether Ukraine has gained the upper hand in the fight against Russia.

clusters.headline: Low Thia Khiang says he will support Pritam Singh ahead of special cadres conference
clusters.summary: The Workers' Party is holding a special cadres conference over the future of secretary-general Pritam Singh, months after he was convicted of lying to a parliamentary committee of privileges.
